mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding output capacity and improving corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of INDPRO month-over-month change vs 20-day return remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or decile spread is non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL above 0.6% signals accelerating inflation, prompting expectations of tighter Fed policy, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Median 5-day forward return following trigger is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), or less than 40% of triggered instances produce negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous low unemployment (UNRATE <= 4%) and strong payroll gains (PAYEMS month-over-month > 200K) indicate an overheating labor market that squeezes corporate margins via wage pressure, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Hit rate of negative 20-day forward returns in triggered periods is below 55%, or rank IC of interaction signal is less than 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a high inflation regime (CPIAUCSL YoY > 5%), rising industrial production amplifies demand-pull inflation fears, leading markets to discount tighter monetary policy; this depresses equity prices, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY > 5% and INDPRO is rising (month-over-month > 0%), 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or less than 55% of such episodes are negative.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) signals strong consumer spending, which drives corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month change vs 20-day return is less than 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or a long-only strategy based on positive RSAFS growth fails to outperform buy-and-hold.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST month-over-month drop > 5%) signals weakening residential investment and broader economic slowdown, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward return following trigger is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), or less than 45% of triggered instances yield negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.75% and industrial production contracts (month-over-month < 0%), the combination of monetary tightening and economic weakening signals recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: early tightening cycle with slowing economy
failure_condition: Of triggered instances, fewer than 55% produce negative 20-day forward returns, or the interaction signal's rank IC over any 2-year period is below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: In an early tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS <= 1% and Fed has begun hiking), a falling unemployment rate (UNRATE month-over-month decline > 0.1pp) fuels wage growth expectations, accelerating rate hike bets and depressing equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: early tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS <= 1% and Fed has begun hiking)
failure_condition: After adjusting for FEDFUNDS trend, 60-day forward returns following UNRATE decline are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's Sharpe ratio over rolling 3-year periods is below 0.2.
